title: Abdominal instrument and method
abstract: A surgical instrument (100) for placement of a movement restriction device (10) for use in a surgical procedure for treating reflux disease in a patient. The instrument comprises a sleeve (113) and a holding device (111) configured to engage the movement restriction device, wherein the holding device is configured to be placed within the sleeve and be displaceable in relation to the sleeve. The instrument further comprises a first handling portion (101) connected to the sleeve, and a second handling portion (102) connected to the holding device. The handling of at least one of the first and second handling portion creates relative displacement of the holding device in relation to the sleeve, which disengages the holding device from the movement restriction device for performing the placement of the movement restriction device.
claims:
Claims 1. A surgical instrument ( 100 ) for abdominal placement of a movement restriction device ( 10 ) , adapted to restrict the movement of the cardia sphincter ( 14 ) , for use in a surgical procedure for treating reflux disease in a patient, the instrument comprising: - a sleeve ( 1 13 ) , - a holding device ( 11 1 ) configured to engage the movement restriction device, wherein the holding device is configured to be placed within the sleeve and be displaceable in relation to the sleeve, - a first handling portion ( 101 ) connected to the sleeve, and - a second handling portion ( 102 ) connected to the holding device, wherein the handling of at least one of the first and second handling portion creates relative displacement of the holding device in relation to the sleeve, which disengages the holding device from the movement restriction device for performing the placement of the movement restriction device. 2. The surgical instrument according to claim 1 , wherein the instrument comprises: a distal portion ( DP ) , configured to enter the body of the patient in use, and a proximal portion ( PP ) , configured to remain outside of the body of the patient in use, 

 wherein the holding device is positioned in the distal portion and the first and second handling portions are positioned in the proximal portion. 3. The surgical instrument according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the holding device is positioned on an elongated member ( 1 14 ) connected to the second handling portion. 4. The surgical instrument according to claim any one of the preceding claims, wherein the holding device comprises a protruding member configured to engage a recess of the movement restriction device. 5. The surgical instrument according to claim any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first handling portion is linearly displaceable in relation to the second handling portion along a primary length axis ( LA ) of the instrument. 6. The surgical instrument according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first handling portion is rotationally displaceable in relation to the second handling portion around a primary length axis of the instrument. 7. The surgical instrument according to claim 6, wherein the first handling portion comprises a threaded portion ( 101T ) and at least one of the second handling portion and the sleeve comprises a corresponding threaded portion ( 113T ) , such that rotational displacement of the first handling portion in relation to the second handling portion creates linear displacement of the first handling portion in relation to the second handling portion, along the primary length axis of the instrument. 8. The surgical instrument according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the distal portion is bent in relation to the primary length axis of the instrument. 9. The surgical instrument according to claim 8, wherein the distal portion is bent more than 20Â° in relation to the primary length axis of the instrument. 10. The surgical instrument according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the distal portion of the sleeve is rigid and a distal portion of the elongated member is flexible. 11. The surgical instrument according to any one of claims 1 - 9, wherein a distal portion of the sleeve is flexible and a distal portion of the elongated member is rigid. 12. The surgical instrument according to any one of claims 1 - 7, wherein the distal portion is flexible such that the distal portion can be bent in relation to the primary length axis of the instrument. 13. The surgical instrument according to claim 12, wherein the distal portion is operably flexible such that the surgeon can bend the distal portion during the surgical procedure. 14. The surgical instrument according to claim 12, wherein the elongated member is operably flexible such that the surgeon can bend the distal portion during the surgical procedure. 15. The surgical instrument according to any one of the preceding claims 12 - 14, wherein the proximal portion further comprises a third handling portion ( 103 ) for operating the operably flexible distal portion during the surgical procedure. 16. The surgical instrument according to claim 15, wherein the third handling portion is linearly displaceable in relation to the second handling portion 

 along a primary length axis of the instrument, for operating the operably flexible distal portion during the surgical procedure. 17. The surgical instrument according to claim 15, wherein the third handling portion is rotationally displaceable in relation to the second handling portion around a primary length axis of the instrument, for operating the operably flexible distal portion during the surgical procedure. 18. The surgical instrument according to claim 17, wherein the third handling portion comprises a threaded portion ( 103T ) and the second handling portion comprises a corresponding threaded portion ( 102T ) , such that rotational displacement of the third handling portion in relation to the second handling portion creates linear displacement of the third handling portion in relation to the second handling portion along the primary length axis of the instrument, for operating the operably flexible distal portion during the surgical procedure. 19. The surgical instrument according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least part of the surgical instrument is configured to be inserted into the abdomen of a patient through a trocar. 20. The surgical instrument according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the sleeve comprises a rigid portion and a flexible portion ( 112 ) configured to bend, and wherein the sleeve is displaceable relative to the holding device, such that the point on the surgical instrument in which the bending may start can be moved. 21. The surgical instrument according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first handling portion ( 101 ) comprises at least one groove or recess adapted to house at least part of the second handling portion ( 102 ) . 22. The surgical instrument according to claim 21, wherein the at least one groove or recess is adapted to allow relative displacement of the second handling portion (102) and the first handling portion (101). 23. The surgical instrument according to claim 3, wherein the holding device is integrated with the elongated member (114) connected to the second handling portion (102). 24. The surgical instrument according to claim 22, wherein the second handling portion (102) is integrated with the elongated member (114). 25. The surgical instrument according to any one of claims 23 ~ 24, wherein the second handling portion is formed of an end of the elongated member (114) distal to the movement restriction device (110). 26. The surgical instrument according to any one of claims 23 ~ 25 wherein the holding device is formed of an end of the elongated member (114) proximal to the movement restriction device (110). 27. The surgical instrument according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least part of the second handling portion (102) has a curved shape. 28. A surgical method for treating reflux disease in a patient by implanting a movement restriction device with use of a surgical instrument comprising: a sleeve, a holding device configured to engage the movement restriction device, wherein the holding device is configured to be placed within the sleeve and be displaceable in relation to the sleeve, 

 the surgical method comprising: making at least one incision in an abdomen of the patient, dissecting in an area comprising a fundus of a stomach, at least partially inserting the surgical instrument holding the movement restriction device in the abdomen of the patient, placing the movement restriction device contacting the fundus of the stomach, on the outside thereof, fixating the movement restriction device to the fundus wall, and disengaging the movement restriction device by displacing the sleeve in relation to the holding device. 29. The surgical method according to claim 21 , wherein the step of fixating the movement restriction device comprises invaginating the movement restriction device in the stomach fundus wall using stomach to stomach sutures or staplers. 30. The surgical method according to any one of claims 21 and 21 , further comprising the step of fixating the stomach to the esophagus using suture of staplers. 31. The surgical method according to any one of claims 21 - 23, wherein a distal portion of the surgical instrument is bent, and wherein the surgical procedure comprises the step of rotating the instrument with the bend for positioning the movement restriction device. 32. The surgical method according to any one of claims 21 - 23, wherein a distal portion of the surgical instrument is operably flexible, and wherein the 

 surgical procedure comprises the step of operating the operably flexible distal portion for positioning the movement restriction device. 33. The surgical method according to any one of claims 21 - 25, wherein the holding device comprises a protruding member and the movement restriction device comprises a recess configured to engage the protruding member, and wherein the step of disengaging the movement restriction device by displacing the sleeve in relation to the holding device comprises disengaging the protruding member from the recess. 34. The surgical method according to any one of claims 21 - 26, wherein the surgical method is a laparoscopic surgical method further comprising at least one of the steps of: - introducing a tube through the abdominal wall, - filling a fluid or gas into the abdominal cavity, - introducing two or more trocars into the abdominal cavity, - introducing a camera into the abdominal cavity through one of the trocars, - dissecting in the abdomen using a laparoscopic dissection instrument, - introducing the surgical instrument into the abdominal cavity through a trocar, - suturing or stapling using a laparoscopic suturing or stapling instrument, and 

 - removing the surgical instrument into the abdominal cavity through a trocar.
